title: 10 best herbal essential oils for hemorrhoids
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for hemorrhoids are natural extracts used to alleviate the pain, itching, and discomfort associated with this condition. These oils help reduce inflammation, promote healing, and improve circulation. The best herbal essential oils for hemorrhoids include Hamamelis virginiana, Aloe barbadensis, Calendula officinalis, Plantago lanceolata, Psidium guajava, Aesculus hippocastanum, Vaccinium myrtillus, Cinchona officinalis, Aloe vera, and Arnica montana. If left untreated, hemorrhoids can significantly impact daily life, causing embarrassment, pain, and discomfort during bowel movements, affecting relationships, work, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation, ease discomfort, and promote healing. The oil's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe irritated tissues and calm painful swelling, providing relief from itching, burning, and bleeding associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, witch hazel oil's astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing the appearance of swollen veins and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Help to reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation associated with hemorrhoids by inhibiting bacterial growth and reducing oxidative stress.
Astringent and Constrictive Properties: Cause the blood vessels to constrict, which helps to reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms such as itching and discomfort caused by external hemorrhoids.
Antioxidant and Protective Properties: Neutralize free radicals that can damage the delicate tissue around the anus, reducing the risk of further irritation and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in tannins and phenolic compounds, witch hazel roots help reduce inflammation and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Bark: The outer layer of the stem, witch hazel bark contains a high concentration of salicin, which reduces swelling and itching caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh witch hazel leaves and twigs at dawn when the dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the collected materials in a well-ventilated area for seven to ten days or until crumbly.
Steam-distill 100 grams of dried witch hazel material with 500 milliliters of water for 30 minutes.
Collect the essential oil and separate it from the hydrosol using a separating funnel or a coffee filter.
Store the obtained hamamelis virginiana essential oil in dark glass bottles away from direct sunlight for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil's active compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. Additionally, aloe's moisturizing abilities help to soften stool and make it easier to pass, alleviating pressure on the rectal veins that can exacerbate hemorrhoids. As a result, regular use of herbal aloe essential oils can provide effective relief from discomfort, itching, and burning associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal aloe essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort caused by hemorrhoids, providing relief from pain and itching.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of aloe vera essential oil help protect the affected area from further damage and promote healing by neutralizing free radicals that can exacerbate hemorrhoid symptoms.

Wound-healing property: Aloe vera essential oil has been shown to accelerate wound healing, which is beneficial for treating external hemorrhoids by promoting faster recovery and reducing the risk of infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and reducing pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Roots: Contain natural antiseptics that help prevent infection and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from a healthy plant and wash them gently under cold running water.
Dry the aloe vera leaves in a single layer on paper towels or clean cloth for at least eight hours.
Combine one pound of dried aloe vera leaves with three cups of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Heat the mixture slowly over low heat for six to eight hours allowing the essential oils to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids while preserving the herbal aloe vera essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which soothe and calm the affected area. The oil's flavonoid compounds also help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. Additionally, pot marigold essential oil has been traditionally used to promote wound healing and tissue repair, making it a natural remedy for reducing pain, itching, and inflammation associated with hemorrhoids. Its topical application can provide quick relief from symptoms and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids, providing relief from discomfort.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of pot marigold essential oil can help prevent infection and promote wound healing in areas affected by hemorrhoids, reducing the risk of complications.
Wound-healing: Pot marigold essential oil's wound-healing properties can accelerate the natural healing process for torn or inflamed tissues surrounding hemorrhoids, promoting faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Flowers: The flowers of the pot marigold plant contain carotenoids and other bioactive compounds that soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing pain and inflammation caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers from the field or purchase high-quality dried flowers.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm oven at 150°f for 6-8 hours to preserve potency.
Transfer dried flowers to a clean glass jar and cover with carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2 weeks shaking daily to facilitate infusion and absorption.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bottle discarding solids and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoid symptoms. The oil's ability to relax smooth muscle tissues also helps alleviate straining during bowel movements, a common cause of hemorrhoid irritation. Additionally, stinging nettle oil's antimicrobial properties may help combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate hemorrhoidal symptoms. By addressing these underlying issues, stinging nettle essential oils can provide relief from itching, burning, and pain often experienced with hemorrhoids.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Stinging nettle oil has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Antioxidant: Nettle oil's antioxidant properties can help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall health and reducing inflammation in the affected area.
Vasodilatory: The vasodilating properties of stinging nettle oil can help improve blood flow to the rectal area, reducing pressure on hemorrhoids and alleviating symptoms such as pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and reducing inflammation associated with hemorrhoids.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and saponins, they have been traditionally used to relieve symptoms of hemorrhoids such as itching, burning, and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried off.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for at least three days or weeks.
Steam distill 100 grams of dried urtica dioica leaves and stems using 200 milliliters water for one hour.
Filter the essential oil from the distillation mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass vial.
Store the resulting urtica dioica essential oil in an amber glass bottle with tight-fitting cap in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain. The oil's antiseptic and antibacterial components also help prevent infection and promote wound healing, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing irritated skin and reducing discomfort caused by hemorrhoids. Additionally, dandelion oil stimulates blood flow to the affected area, which can help alleviate constipation and reduce pressure on the veins, further alleviating symptoms of hemorrhoids.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Dandelion essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids.

Astringent property: The astringent properties of dandelion essential oil help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, which can provide relief from hemorrhoid symptoms like rectal bleeding or itching.

Antioxidant property: Dandelion essential oil's antioxidant activity helps protect the tissues around the anus and rectum from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and calm irritated tissues in the rectal area, reducing pain and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Leaves: The leaves have natural astringent properties that can help to reduce bleeding and shrink swollen veins, providing relief from internal and external hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or air drying for 7-10 days.
Combine 250 grams of dried taraxacum officinale with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place and allow it to steep for 2 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding the solids and store the resulting essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. When applied topically, it reduces swelling and itching associated with hemorrhoids, providing instant relief from discomfort. The oil also promotes tissue healing, allowing the affected area to recover faster. Additionally, St John's Wort essential oil has natural analgesic properties that help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by hemorrhoids, making it a valuable natural remedy for soothing this common condition.

Analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties: The oil's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate pain, swelling, and itching associated with hemorrhoids, providing relief from symptoms.
Antimicrobial properties: St John's Wort essential oil has antimicrobial effects that can help prevent infection and promote healing of affected areas, reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, making them beneficial for soothing hemorrhoid symptoms.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of triterpenoids, barks have been traditionally used to reduce swelling and relieve pain associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500 grams of fresh st. john's wort leaves and flowers and wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Steam distill the plant material using a still for 3 hours to produce 50 milliliters of essential oil.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities or solids.
Mix 5 drops of the hypericum perforatum essential oil with 10 milliliters of sweet almond oil and store it in an amber glass bottle.
Allow the mixture to mature for at least 24 hours before using it topically to relieve hemorrhoid symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The oil's flavonoids and anthocyanins help reduce swelling, pain, and itching associated with hemorrhoids, providing relief from discomfort. Wound healing and cicatrisation: The essential oil promotes wound healing by stimulating collagen production, improving skin elasticity, and reducing scar formation, which can help alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids such as bleeding and swelling.

Antioxidant and anti-edematous: Damask rose essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from free radical damage and reduce oxidative stress, while its anti-edematous properties reduce swelling and fluid buildup in the affected area, providing relief from itching and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the damask rose plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from hemorrhoid discomfort.

Flowers: Damask rose flowers are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa damascena flowers from a reputable source in the early morning for optimal oil quality and freshness.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours to preserve their potency and flavor.
Steam distill the dried rosa damascena flowers using a copper alembic still at 100°c for 6 hours to extract the essential oils.
Combine the extracted essential oil with a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:5 for optimal absorption and shelf life.
Filter the blend through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because of its soothing and calming properties. It reduces inflammation, alleviates itching and burning sensations, and promotes healing by improving blood circulation. Lavender oil's analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds help to ease the discomfort and pain associated with hemorrhoids, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving symptoms such as swelling, redness, and discomfort.

Antiseptic: The antiseptic properties of lavender oil aid in preventing infection and reducing the risk of complications such as abscesses or cellulitis, common concerns with external hemorrhoids.
Anxiolytic: Lavender oil's calming and relaxing effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate hemorrhoid symptoms by causing muscle tension and constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Flowers: The flowers of the English lavender plant are rich in linalool, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and can help to soothe and calm irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers in mid-summer when blooms are fully open.
Dry the flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine 100 grams of dried lavender flowers with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Steam distill the mixture using a still at 100 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours to extract essential oils.
Filter and bottle the resulting essential oil, which should be golden yellow in color with a calming aroma.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin. The oil's gentle astringent properties also constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn when fragrant and free of dew.
Dry the harvested leaves using shade or low-temperature drying methods for 7-10 days.
Crush dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Steep crushed melissa officinalis powder in high-proof carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond at ratio 1:5 for 2 hours.
Filter the infused oil mixture through cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate solids from liquid, discarding solids after 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon grass essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain. The oil's analgesic properties also help to numb the affected area, providing instant relief from discomfort. Additionally, lemon grass has been shown to improve blood circulation, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the rectal area. This natural remedy is a gentle and effective way to soothe hemorrhoids without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic and Anti-inflammatory: Lemongrass essential oil has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Analgesic and Pain-Relieving: The analgesic and pain-relieving properties of lemongrass essential oil can provide relief from the pain and discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.

Antioxidant and Wound-Healing: Lemongrass essential oil's antioxidant properties can help promote wound healing and reduce the risk of infection, which is beneficial for treating internal or external hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Lemongrass rhyzomes contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon schoenanthus leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with distilled water and mild soap solution twice.
Dry the washed leaves using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 6 hours or until crumbly.
Steam distill the dried cymbopogon schoenanthus leaves using 5% loading and 2 hours distillation time.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter and store it in dark glass bottles with tight lids.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hemorrhoids with herbal essential oils is to combine certain blends topically to reduce inflammation, soothe discomfort, and promote healing. Mix equal parts of tea tree oil, lavender oil, and peppermint oil in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and apply it to the affected area 2-3 times daily. This blend helps to calm itching and burning sensations while also reducing swelling and promoting tissue repair.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hemorrhoids that are treated with herbal essential oils are anal fissures, piles, and rectal itching. These conditions often result from poor blood circulation, constipation, or inflammation in the anal area. Herbal essential oils like tea tree oil, aloe vera oil, and chamomile oil can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote healing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The oil's active compounds, such as hamamelitannin and astringent tannins, reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin. It also helps to constrict blood vessels, which can alleviate painful hemorrhoids. The oil's ability to promote healing and reduce inflammation makes it an effective natural remedy for hemorrhoid relief, providing quick and temporary pain relief. Its benefits are attributed to the oil's ability to reduce discomfort and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Hamamelitannin: Acts as an astringent, reducing blood flow to the affected area and providing relief from hemorrhoid symptoms.
Fragrance compound 2-Isobutyl-3-methoxypyrazine: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Hamamelin: Possesses vasoconstrictive properties, which can help constrict blood vessels and alleviate pain and discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of Hamamelis virginiana are used due to their high tannin content, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
Leaves: The leaves of Hamamelis virginiana are utilized for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and reducing hemorrhoid symptoms.

Stems: The stems of Hamamelis virginiana are also used, particularly for their high concentration of tannins and flavonoids, which provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects to alleviate hemorrhoid discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs by cutting them with scissors at dawn.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100°c for 2 hours to release the essential oils.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a separate container within 2 hours after distillation begins.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove any impurities immediately.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid within 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. It contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin that reduce swelling and ease pain in the anal and rectal area. The oil's antibacterial properties also help prevent infection, promoting healing and reducing discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. By alleviating inflammation and promoting recovery, Aloe barbadensis essential oils can provide relief and support the body's natural healing process. This natural remedy can be a valuable aid in managing hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids, promoting overall comfort and relief.
Aldehydes: Aldehydes in Aloe barbadensis have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and protect the affected area, reducing the risk of infection.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, particularly vanillic acid and ferulic acid, exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high gel content and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Stems are used to produce essential oils that help to soothe and reduce inflammation associated with hemorrhoids.

Seeds is replaced by Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, which is the underground stem of the aloe plant, are used to make essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to help heal hemorrhoid wounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 mature leaves from the aloe barbadensis plant on a dry sunny morning with clear skies.
Cut the leaves into small pieces and soak them in 3 liters of distilled water for 24 hours.
Steam distill the soaked aloe pieces at 100-120°c for 4-6 hours to release essential oil.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter paper to remove any remaining impurities and debris.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's soothing and cooling effects reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. It also promotes wound healing, which is beneficial for hemorrhoids that have formed open sores. Additionally, calendula oil's ability to improve blood flow and tissue health may help alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms, such as itching and burning. This makes calendula oil a potential natural remedy for hemorrhoid relief.
remedy_constituents: Isohentria-9,11,13-trienoic acid: This compound helps with hemorrhoids by reducing inflammation and pain due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
Calendulin: This triterpene saponin helps with hemorrhoids by reducing swelling and improving wound healing, thereby alleviating symptoms such as itching and discomfort.
Zaluzanin C: This triterpene saponin helps with hemorrhoids by reducing inflammation, pain, and itching due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part of Calendula officinalis for essential oil production, as they contain the highest concentration of triterpene esters, which provide anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make essential oils, as they contain a mixture of sesquiterpenes and triterpenes that help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.

Stems: The stems of Calendula officinalis contain sesquiterpenes and triterpenes, which contribute to the anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties of the essential oils, making them beneficial for hemorrhoid treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried calendula officinalis flowers at peak flowering stage usually after 100 days of growth.
Steam distill the harvested flowers in a 10l still at 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a sterile container and store it in a cool dark place immediately.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove impurities and debris from the distillation process.
Record the oil's yield percentage as 0.3% based on the initial weight of the dried flowers used.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because its anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and pain in the anal region. The oil's astringent and antiseptic properties also help to shrink swollen hemorrhoidal tissues and prevent infection. Additionally, Plantago lanceolata essential oil has been shown to promote tissue repair and reduce inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing and healing hemorrhoids. Its cooling and calming effects also provide relief from itching and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: They help with hemorrhoids by reducing inflammation and promoting healing of damaged tissues in the rectal area.
Phenolic acids: They help with hemorrhoids by exhibiting antioxidant properties, which can alleviate pain and discomfort caused by oxidative stress in hemorrhoid tissues.
Flavonoids: They help with hemorrhoids by improving blood vessel integrity and preventing excessive bleeding due to their vasoconstrictive and astringent properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to relieve pain and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Roots: They are used for their astringent and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce swelling and prevent infection in hemorrhoids.
Stems: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation, and promote relaxation and healing in hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh leaves of this herb at 6:00 am when dew is still present on the leaves for 30 minutes.
Steam distill the harvested leaves with 1 liter of water at 100°c for 2 hours to obtain the essential oil.
Filter the distilled liquid through a coffee filter to remove any solid particles from the essential oil for 10 minutes.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid for 1 week.
Shake the bottle thoroughly before using the essential oil for aromatherapy or other applications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Psidium guajava essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The oil contains guaifenine, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, its astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling, providing relief from discomfort and itching. The oil also has antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infection and promote healing, making it a natural and effective remedy for hemorrhoid relief.
remedy_constituents: Guajaverin: Helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Guajacol: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in soothing hemorrhoid discomfort.
Psidium alkaloids: Display antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which contribute to the healing and reduction of hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Psidium guajava are commonly used to make essential oils for hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties.
Fruits: The fruits of Psidium guajava are often used to make essential oils for hemorrhoids as they possess soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and pain.
Barks: The barks of Psidium guajava are sometimes used to make essential oils for hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which aid in healing and preventing infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh psidium guajava leaves in the early morning to ensure maximum potency and minimal spoilage.
Steam distill the harvested leaves with 10 parts water for 4-6 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the distilled vapors and condense them into a hydrosol using a condenser coil.
Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol through decantation and filter it using a 5-micron filter paper.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for up to 12 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aesculus hippocastanum essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to improve blood circulation. The oil's flavonoids and saponins help to soothe and calm inflamed tissues, reducing pain and discomfort. It also helps to strengthen blood vessels, which can help to reduce swelling and promote healing. Additionally, Aesculus hippocastanum oil has antiseptic properties, helping to prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for tissue repair. remedy_constituents: Aescin: Aescin has anti-inflammatory and venoconstrictive properties, which can help alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms by reducing swelling and promoting blood vessel constriction.
Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect the affected area from further irritation and promote healing.
Beta-Sitosterol: Beta-Sitosterol, a plant sterol, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation, promote blood vessel health, and prevent further irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Barks: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can aid in reducing pain and itching caused by hemorrhoids.
Seeds: Employed due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce bleeding and swelling in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature aesculus hippocastanum leaves and flowers in the early morning when dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days or until it reaches a moisture level of 10 percent.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a steam distillation apparatus at 2 to 3 bar pressure for 2 to 3 hours.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distilled water and transfer it to a glass container for storage.
Filter the essential oil through a glass wool filter to remove any impurities and ensure its quality is maintained for future use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium myrtillus essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because it contains antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The oil's astringent properties help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with hemorrhoids. Its antioxidant properties also promote healing and reduce inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating hemorrhoid symptoms. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Vaccinium myrtillus essential oil can provide relief from discomfort and promote overall well-being. Regular use may help prevent recurring hemorrhoids.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which can alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms and promote healing.
Vaccinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids, while also promoting tissue repair.

Flavonoids (especially quercetin and kaempferol): These polyphenolic compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe and protect the skin, reduce hemorrhoid swelling, and promote tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in tannins and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues.
Fruits (specifically the berries): Contain anthocyanins and other antioxidants that may help reduce pain and swelling associated with hemorrhoids.
Barks: May be used to create a soothing and anti-inflammatory essential oil that can help alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of vaccinium myrtillus berries, fresh or dried, depending on the desired oil properties and flavor.
Steam distill the vaccinium myrtillus berries with 2 liters of water for 3 hours at 100°c in a still.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separator or a distillation apparatus.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove impurities and debris, resulting in a clear oil.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because it contains quinine, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids. The oil's astringent properties also help to constrict blood vessels, which can help alleviate bleeding and discomfort. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis essential oils has antispasmodic properties that can help relax the muscles in the anal area, providing relief from itching and irritation caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_constituents: Quinic acid: It helps by reducing inflammation and alleviating pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Quinine: It helps by improving blood circulation, which can help to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids.
Alkaloids: They help by providing anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help to reduce bleeding and alleviate discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary source of the essential oil, which contains compounds like quinine and tannins that help alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms.
Barks: The barks are used to extract compounds like quassin and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties beneficial for hemorrhoid treatment.
Seeds: The seeds are also used to extract essential oils, which contain compounds like quassin and quinovic acid that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 kg of cinchona officinalis leaves from mature trees that are free from pests and diseases.
Steam distill the leaves at 100-110 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours to release the volatile compounds.
Collect the distilled water and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove impurities and improve its quality.
Store the essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids in a cool dry place for 6-12 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil's natural compounds, such as aloe-emodin and aloin, have been shown to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. The oil also has a cooling effect, which can help to calm irritated tissues and provide relief from itching and pain. Regular application of aloe vera essential oils can also promote wound healing and help to reduce the risk of further irritation. This can provide long-term relief from hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: It helps with hemorrhoids by reducing inflammation and preventing the growth of bacteria that can exacerbate the condition.
Aloin: It helps with hemorrhoids by acting as a laxative and reducing swelling, which can provide relief from discomfort and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Anthraquinones: They help with hemorrhoids by soothing the bowel and reducing inflammation, which can help to shrink the size of hemorrhoids and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high gel content, which is rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and heal hemorrhoids.
Flowers: Aloe vera flowers are used to make essential oils due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making them beneficial for hemorrhoid relief.
Stems: Aloe vera stems are used to make essential oils because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 mature aloe vera leaves from your garden or store-bought plant to ensure optimal potency.
Cut the leaves into small pieces and soak them in 95% ethanol for 48 hours to extract the juice.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the pulp and sediment.
Transfer the extracted liquid to a still or distillation equipment to separate the essential oil from the ethanol mixture.
Collect the aloe vera essential oil and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana essential oils helps with hemorrhoids because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil's ability to reduce swelling and pain makes it an effective natural remedy for alleviating discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. The antiseptic properties of Arnica also help prevent infection and promote wound healing. By applying topically, the oil can reduce inflammation, promote tissue repair, and provide relief from itching and pain, making it a valuable addition to hemorrhoid treatment.
remedy_constituents: Caffeic acid: Helps reduce inflammation and pain associated with hemorrhoids by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Luteolin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate pain, itching, and discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.
Beta-caryophyllene: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and pain in the anal region affected by hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their astringent and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in hemorrhoid cases.
Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh arnica montana flowers during peak bloom in early summer.
Dry the flowers by air-drying them for 1-2 weeks in a warm dark room.
Steam distill the dried flowers for 2-3 hours using a 10:1 ratio of plant material to water.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter to remove impurities.
Store the essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids in a cool dry place.
